rationale: Increasing the homestead exemption reduces taxable property values, shrinking the ad valorem tax base. This interacts with §45.261(c) and (e), which tie reimbursement revenue and voter-approval tax rate calculations to that tax base, potentially requiring consequential statutory amendments to maintain intended reimbursement mechanics.
quote:
(c) The commissioner may order a school district to set an ad valorem tax rate capable of producing an amount of revenue sufficient to enable the district to:
(1) provide reimbursement under this section; and
(2) pay the remaining principal of and interest on the bonds as the principal and interest become due.
_note: Proposed change does not directly amend §45.261 but reduces the ad valorem tax base that underlies the revenue-production and voter-approval tax rate mechanics in subsections (c) and (e), creating a consequential interaction likely requiring statutory adjustment.
